questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the functional distinction between 'Niryat Protsahan' and 'Niryat Disha' under the Export Promotion Mission?

options.text: Protsahan targets large corporate houses, while Disha is exclusively for the MSME sector.
Protsahan provides financial support for affordable trade finance, while Disha provides non-financial support for market readiness.
Protsahan is a permanent statutory body, while Disha is a temporary scheme under the DGFT.
Protsahan focuses on the export of services, while Disha focuses on the export of physical goods.

questions.explanation: The EPM is structured such that Niryat Protsahan handles the financial aspects of trade facilitation (affordable finance), whereas Niryat Disha handles the capacity-building and readiness aspects (non-financial support).
questions.takeaway: The EPM uses a dual-track approach: financial facilitation (Protsahan) and capacity building (Disha).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Niryat Protsahan provides financial support for affordable trade finance, while Niryat Disha provides non-financial support to enhance market readiness.
